title: Stelis cosangae
intro: Stelis cosangae, also known as the Stelis species, is a type of orchid plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Stelis cosangae are attributed to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelis cosangae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Stelis cosangae are teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Stelis cosangae is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This species belongs to the genus Stelis, which comprises over 900 species of orchids. The genus Stelis is further divided into several subgenera, and Stelis cosangae falls within one of these subgroups. More specifically, Stelis cosangae is classified as part of the subgenus Pleurothallis. The classification of Stelis cosangae is subject to ongoing research and potential revisions in the field of orchid taxonomy.
